svg.text: A Dictionary is a data structure used to represent a set of mapping relationships. Each Dictionary
defines a mapping from data type A (Key) to data type B (Value). Each Key+Value is a Key-Value Pair.
Store player scores on a Stage Entity without needing multiple Custom Variables.
Map Entity to Integer: Entity stores Player, Integer records Score. Modify elements via Dictionary Operation Nodes.
Can be used in Server Node Graphs. Can be packaged as a data type for use and transfer.
Strongly typed. Type uniquely defined by Key and
Value types. [String to Float] cannot connect to
No duplicate Keys. Each Key must be unique.
Like Lists, passed by reference. Modification nodes
directly affect data in Custom/Node Graph Variables.
Pre/Post-Change Value not provided in events.
Key-Value Pairs not stored in order. Treat as unordered.
III. Available Data Types for Dictionaries
Entity, GUID, Integer, Boolean, Floating Point Numbers,
String, Faction, 3D Vector, Prefab ID, Configuration ID,
All corresponding List types also supported as Values.
Dictionary is a special type of Generic Pin.
Type is only fully defined once both Key and Value
Set or Add Key Value Pair
Add or update a key-value pair in the dictionary.
Sort by Key / Sort by Value
Sort dictionary entries by key or value.
Get value associated with a specific key.
Extract all keys or all values as a list.
Check if dictionary contains specific key or value.
Create new dictionary or assemble from parts.
A Structure is an advanced data type that groups different kinds of data into a single piece of
custom data. Each Structure has a unique name and can contain multiple data entries (Members).
Name (String), ATK (Floating Point Numbers), Enhanced or Not (Boolean). Can be used as Custom Variable,
combined with Dictionary (e.g., Integer ID to Weapon Data) to create a weapon database.
Type determined by name. Even identical layouts
are different if names differ. No duplicate names.
Structures can contain other Structures and Lists.
Like Lists, passed by reference. Modification nodes
directly affect data in Custom/Node Graph Variables.
All Basic, List, Dictionary, and Structure types.
Menu > Advanced Data Management > Structure Editing Tab. View existing structures, add new ones,
edit member values. Add new structures and add new members within structures.
After creation, use defined structures in Server Node Graph, Custom Variables, and Node Graph Variables.
Structures and Structure Lists are special Generic Pin types. Select [Structure] or [Structure List] in
the Generic dropdown. Expanding shows all Structure types defined in the Stage.
Fill in values for each parameter to generate Structure data.
Select Structure type via Generic button first. Displays all Members.
Pass in a Structure to obtain values of each Member.
Select Structure type via Generic button first. Displays all Members.
Edit values of selected Members within Structure data.
Use dropdown to choose which Members to edit. Only checked
Members are modified. Unselected Members remain unchanged.
1. Create collectible prefabs with name variables
2. Add Collision Trigger to each item
3. Create Dictionary (String to Integer) on Stage Entity
5. Node graph: query dictionary by item name, update
player score, display UI text, destroy item
1. Create equipment prefabs with Level variable
2. Define Structure: ATK, DEF, HP (Float base values)
3. Add Collision Trigger for pickup event
4. Add player variables: Equipment Level, ATK/DEF/HP
5. Node graph: get Level, Split Structure for base values,
multiply Level x base = final bonus, update player
Combine Dictionary and Structure for complex data: e.g., Dictionary mapping Integer IDs to Weapon Data Structures
creates a complete weapon database. Both are passed by reference, so modifications affect stored variables directly.
